title: Instrumented trail making task (itmt)
abstract: An instrumented trail-making task (iTMT) platform includes a wearable sensor and interactive interface technology configured to identify cognitive-cognitive impairment in individuals such as older adults. The iTMT platform may be programmed with neuropsychological tests for assessing individuals. The iTMT may provide information on visual search, scanning, speed of processing, mental flexibility, and/or executive functions as well as physical biomarkers of motor performance including slowness, weakness, exclusion, and/or motor planning error. Results of tests administered by the iTMT system may be reported to a patient or caregiver and used in identifying cognitive-motor impairment among individuals suffering from cognitive impairment, dementia, and/or those with frailty status, and/or cognitive frailty, and/or high risk of falling, and/or high likelihood of decline in cognitive-motor over time.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: A method, comprising: presenting, by a processor on a display, a series of requested motions to be performed by an individual; receiving, by the processor, motion data from a sensor recording the individual performing the series of requested motions; analyzing, by the processor, the received motion data to determine a cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual; and reporting, by the processor, the cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of presenting the series of requested motions comprises presenting a series of sequentially-ordered numbers in a fixed order on a display. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of presenting the series of requested motions comprises presenting a series of sequentially-ordered numbers in a random order on a display. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of presenting the series of requested motions comprises presenting a series of sequentially-ordered numbers and letters in a random order on a display. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of presenting the series of requested motions comprises presenting a series of objects on a display. 

 The method of claim 1, wherein the step of receiving data from the sensor comprises receiving motion data for a body-joint that moves a cursor on the display through the series of requested motions. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data comprises determining a total time used by the individual to complete the series of requested motions. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data comprises: counting a number of mistakes in performing the requested motions to determine a working memory performance; and measuring a time elapsed between onset of movement and onset of display of virtual targets on the screen to estimate the ability of visual search, scanning, speed of processing, mental flexibility, and/or executive functions. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data to determine a cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual comprises analyzing a peak velocity during point-to-point reaching to assess motor performance or frailty. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data to determine a cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual comprises analyzing a dislocation of peak velocity with respect to a middle pathway of a point-to-point reaching task to assess cognitive impairment, motor impairment, or fatigue. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data to determine a cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual comprises 

 analyzing a jerkiness of point-to-point reaching kinematic parameters to assess weakness and motor performance. The method of claim 1, wherein the step of reporting the cognitive-motor impairment score comprises determining whether an individual exhibits signs of cognitive impairment, motor impairment, physical frailty, cognitive frailty, or a combination thereof. A computer program product a non-transitory computer readable medium comprising code for performing steps comprising: presenting a series of requested motions to be performed by an individual; receiving motion data from a sensor recording the individual performing the series of requested motions; analyzing the received motion data to determine a cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual; and reporting the cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual. The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of presenting the series of requested motions comprises presenting a series of sequentially-ordered numbers in a fixed order on a display. The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of presenting the series of requested motions comprises presenting a series of sequentially-ordered numbers in a random order on a display. 

 The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of presenting the series of requested motions comprises presenting a series of sequentially-ordered numbers and letters in a random order on a display. The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of presenting the series of requested motions comprises presenting a series of objects on a display. The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of receiving data from the sensor comprises receiving motion data for an ankle-joint that moves a cursor on a display screen through the series of requested motions. The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data comprises determining a total time used by the individual to complete the series of requested motions. The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data comprises: counting a number of mistakes in performing the requested motions to determine a working memory performance; and measuring a time elapsed between onset of movement and onset of display of virtual targets on the screen to estimate the ability of visual search, scanning, speed of processing, mental flexibility, and/or executive functions. The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of reporting the cognitive-motor impairment score comprises determining whether an individual exhibits signs of Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) or Alzheimer's Disease (AD). The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data to determine a cognitive-motor impairment score for the 

 individual comprises analyzing a peak velocity during point-to-point reaching to assess motor performance or frailty. The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data to determine a cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual comprises analyzing a dislocation of peak velocity with respect to a middle pathway of a point-to-point reaching task to assess cognitive impairment, motor impairment, or fatigue. The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data to determine a cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual comprises analyzing a jerkiness of point-to-point reaching kinematic parameters to assess weakness and motor performance. The computer program product of claim 13, wherein the step of reporting the cognitive-motor impairment score comprises determining whether an individual exhibits signs of cognitive impairment, motor impairment, physical frailty, cognitive frailty, or a combination thereof. A system, comprising: a wearable sensor comprising at least one motion sensor, wherein the wearable sensor is configured to record motion data from the at least one motion sensor and to transmit the motion data; and a computing device configured to receive the motion data transmitted by the wearable sensor and configured to perform steps comprising: presenting a series of requested motions to be performed by an individual; receiving motion data from the wearable sensor recording the individual performing the series of requested motions; 

 analyzing the received motion data to determine a cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual; and reporting the cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual. 27. The system of claim 26, wherein the step of presenting the series of requested motions comprises presenting a series of sequentially-ordered numbers and letters in a random order on a spatial display. 31. The system of claim 26, wherein the step of presenting the series of requested motions comprises presenting a series of objects on a display. 32. The system of claim 26, wherein the step of analyzing the received motion data to determine a cognitive-motor impairment score for the individual comprises analyzing a dislocation of peak velocity with respect to a middle pathway of a point-to-point reaching task to assess cognitive impairment, motor impairment, or fatigue. 35. The system of claim 26, wherein the processing device is integrated with the wearable sensor.
